evidence: SemanticFieldExplorerTest, MultiSeedExplorerTest, SemanticFieldExplorerInternalTest, CollocateProfileComparatorTest — full algorithm coverage with stub executors
GrammarConfigLoaderTest, RelationsValidationTest, RelationUtilsTest — config loading and validation covered
ExplorationHandlersTest (3 tests: happy-path for explore, explore-multi, compare) added recently
VisualizationHandlersTest (4 tests: mode error, body too large, invalid JSON, successful render)
HandlersTest covers error paths for SketchHandlers (missing lemma → 400) and ConcordanceHandlers (missing params → 400)
SketchHandlers has no happy-path unit tests — full sketch response shape, single-relation sketch, dep sketch response format are all untested at the handler level
ConcordanceHandlers.handleConcordanceExamples has only error-path tests in HandlersTest; no test verifies the response body structure for a valid request
ExplorationHandlersTest.handleSemanticFieldComparison_twoSeeds_returns200 only asserts body != null — no verification of response keys (adjectives, seeds, parameters)
BlackLab live-index tests (BlackLabQueryExecutorIntegrationTest, HybridAlgorithmTest, DependencySketchTest) are @Disabled — documented as wontfix

strengths: Algorithm classes (SemanticFieldExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, CollocateProfileComparator) have thorough unit tests using stub executors — the core logic is well-validated without a real index
ExploreResponseAssemblerTest validates edge building, response key population, and multi-seed edge sourcing — critical serialization logic is tested
VisualizationHandlersTest covers all four key paths (valid render, too-large body, invalid JSON, unknown mode) — good boundary coverage for a POST handler
GrammarConfigLoaderTest and RelationsValidationTest cover config parsing, validation errors, and alias resolution thoroughly
HttpApiUtilsTest and WrapHandlerTest cover the shared CORS, error-mapping, and method-enforcement infrastructure
test_strategy.issue_character: The test gap is concentrated in the SketchHandlers and ConcordanceHandlers happy paths — the API layer's most-used endpoints lack any test verifying that a valid request produces a structurally correct response, leaving response shape regressions undetectable without a live index.
test_strategy.score_rationale: The exploration and config test coverage is strong. The recent ExplorationHandlersTest and VisualizationHandlersTest additions improve handler coverage. However SketchHandlers — which handles the four main word-sketch endpoints — has no happy-path test at all: a developer refactoring handleFullSketchForType or the relation-response format has no unit-level regression signal. ConcordanceHandlers similarly. One of the three new ExplorationHandlers tests (comparison) is too weak to catch response-shape regressions. This collection of gaps is enough to score at 74 (a developer would worry about changes in certain areas) rather than 80 (strategic blind spots) — the sketch handler gap is both large and at the highest-traffic endpoint.

issues.summary: SketchHandlers has no happy-path unit tests — full sketch, single-relation, and dep sketch response shapes are untested at the handler level
evidence: HandlersTest tests only the missing-lemma 400 path for SketchHandlers — no test constructs a SketchHandlers with a stub executor and verifies the response body
handleFullSketchForType, handleRelationSketch, handleFullDependencySketch, and handleRelationQueryForPattern all have zero happy-path test coverage at the handler level
ExplorationHandlersTest shows the pattern for adding stub-executor handler tests — the same approach applies to SketchHandlers
issues.suggestion: Add a SketchHandlersTest class following the ExplorationHandlersTest pattern: create a stub QueryExecutor returning canned WordSketchResult lists, invoke handleSketchRequest (full sketch), handleRelationSketch, and handleFullDependencySketch, and assert: status=200, response contains 'status'='ok', 'lemma' is echoed, 'relations' is a non-null map. This verifies response shape without a real index. Three tests covering the main dispatch branches would provide meaningful regression protection.

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldComparison test only asserts body != null, missing response structure verification
evidence: Test: 'assertNotNull(body, "Response body should be valid JSON")' — no assertion on 'status', 'seeds', 'adjectives', or 'parameters' keys
Sister tests (handleSemanticFieldExplore and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti) both assert on specific response fields
The comparison endpoint has a different response shape (no relation_type, different top-level keys) that could regress silently with this minimal assertion
issues.suggestion: Strengthen the comparison test: after parsing the body, assert: 'assertEquals("ok", body.getString("status"))', 'assertNotNull(body.getJSONArray("adjectives"))', 'assertNotNull(body.getJSONArray("seeds"))', 'assertNotNull(body.getJSONObject("parameters"))'. These three assertions mirror the level of coverage the other two exploration handler tests provide.

root_causes: SketchHandlers predates the ExplorationHandlersTest pattern and was never back-filled with happy-path tests
Comparison test was written to match the existing structure but the assertions were not expanded to match the test's siblings
possible_false_positives: BlackLab live-index @Disabled tests are intentional wontfixes and should not be flagged as coverage gaps
